risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "randomisation within strata, this will be generated using random number generating software" Comment: The study explicitly states that ASUs were stratified and then the allocation sequence was generated using random number generating software by an independent offshore statistician, which is a truly random method.

Quote: "Stratification details will be provided in a de-identified form to an independent statistician located offshore and not otherwise involved in the study for randomisation within strata... Allocation will be based on clusters (ASUs) rather than individuals, and the sequence will be concealed until the intervention is assigned." Comment: The report explicitly states that an independent offshore statistician performed the randomisation and that the sequence was concealed until assignment, which is sufficient evidence that recruiters could not foresee upcoming allocation.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Both the medical director and NUM of all consenting ASUs will be aware that our study is examining the effect of an intervention... medical directors and NUMs from ASUs subsequently randomised to the control group may be able to deduce their group allocation because no workshops are being organised." Also, "Patients will be blinded to group allocation." Comment: This was a cluster trial of an organisational/educational intervention delivered to stroke units, with intervention sites receiving workshops, education, reminders, and support. Personnel delivering care in intervention units could not be blinded, and control-site leaders may deduce allocation. Because the main outcomes include care processes and patient outcomes that could plausibly be influenced by clinicians' knowledge of allocation, this domain is at high risk overall.

Quote: "All CATIs will be undertaken by research interviewers blind to the study design and also to ASU group allocation" and "Data entry will be undertaken by the CATI research assistants blind to group allocation." Also, "Data will be obtained by retrospective medical record audit undertaken by independent research assistants (IRAs) blind to group allocation." Comment: The main patient outcomes (mRS, Barthel Index, SF-36, death/disability) were assessed by blinded telephone interviewers, and chart-audit outcomes were assessed by blinded independent research assistants. These outcomes are partly subjective, so blinding of assessors is important and explicitly reported; therefore detection bias is unlikely.

Quote: "For missing data, patient clinical data will be obtained from the TASC database. Patients themselves will already have agreed to allow access to these data as part of the study consent." Comment: The protocol indicates a planned strategy to retrieve missing clinical data from an external database, and it specifies intention-to-treat analysis. It also anticipates only 10% loss to follow-up in sample size calculations, suggesting attrition was expected to be modest rather than likely to bias results.
